units.title: Form 1: Introduction to Hospitality
learningOutcomes: Apply traditional and modern hospitality practices in Zambia
Explore benefits of the hospitality industry
Analyse departments of the hospitality facility
suggestedActivities: Discussing hospitality industry sectors (lodging, food and beverage, travel and tourism, recreation and entertainment, event management)
Explaining the history of the hospitality industry (ancient times, middle ages, renaissance, enlightenment)
Describing the link between hospitality and tourism (hotels and resorts, restaurants and bars, tour operations, travel agencies)
Identifying job and career opportunities in the hospitality business (hotel manager, receptionist, chef, event coordinator, entrepreneur)
Undertaking an educational tour to a hospitality facility
Practicing traditional and modern social, cultural, organizational and group hospitality norms
Discussing departments in a hospitality facility (front office, housekeeping, food and beverage, food production, supporting departments)
assessmentMethods: Understanding of the historical background of the hospitality industry demonstrated appropriately
Traditional and modern hospitality practices in Zambia applied accordingly
Departments of the hospitality facility analysed appropriately

units.title: Form 1: Front Office Operations
learningOutcomes: Perform roles of the front office
Utilize front office equipment
suggestedActivities: Identifying front office personnel (front office manager, receptionist, concierge, porters)
Discussing personal hygiene and general appearance of front office personnel
Explaining characteristics of front office personnel (communication skills, positive attitude, professionalism, teamwork)
Performing front office roles (guest reservation, guest registration, room assignment)
Identifying and making use of front office equipment (property management system, point of sale, key card system, printer)
Classifying front office security and surveillance equipment (alarm system, CCTV camera, fireproof safe)
assessmentMethods: Roles of the front office performed correctly
Front office equipment utilized correctly

units.title: Form 1: Food and Beverage Operations
topics: Hygiene Practices in the Cooking Area
Safety and Security in the Cooking Area
learningOutcomes: Demonstrate understanding of food and beverage operations
Demonstrate understanding of hygiene practices
Practice safety and security in the cooking area
Apply nutritional knowledge in cookery
Demonstrate understanding of cookery and apply a variety of methods of cooking
Exhibit basic cookery skills
suggestedActivities: Defining food and beverage operations and discussing the organizational structure (kitchen brigade, restaurant personnel, bar personnel)
Describing kitchen, personal and food hygiene practices in the cooking area
Making use of safe work practices (personal protective equipment, knife safety, fire safety, electrical safety, emergency procedures) and describing safety signs and symbols
Discussing nutrition terminologies, nutrient functions and sources, meal planning and diets for different lifestyles
Classifying kitchen equipment and utensils, including traditional utensils and equipment
Making use of moist, dry and combination methods of cooking (steaming, boiling, stewing, roasting, grilling, stir frying)
Practicing knife skills, stock making, thickening agents and basic sauces
assessmentMethods: Understanding of food and beverage operations demonstrated correctly
Safety and security in the cooking area practiced effectively
Nutritional knowledge in cookery applied suitably
A variety of methods of cooking applied efficiently
Basic cookery skills exhibited methodically

units.title: Form 1: Housekeeping Operations
topics: Cleanliness, Hygiene and Safety in the Housekeeping Department
Interior and Exterior Decoration in a Hospitality Facility
learningOutcomes: Practice housekeeping operations
Practice routine cleaning
Develop skills in interior and exterior decorations
suggestedActivities: Identifying and eradicating pests in hospitality facilities (chemical, biological, cultural and physical control)
Practicing safety precautions and handling waste in housekeeping (reduce, reuse, recycling)
Planning routine cleaning schedules (daily, weekly, monthly) and identifying cleaning areas
Applying different methods of cleaning (dusting, sweeping, mopping, scrubbing) and using cleaning agents
Making a bed and demonstrating steps in bed making
Making use of colour schemes and applying principles and elements of decoration
Practicing landscaping and gardening
assessmentMethods: Cleanliness, hygiene and safety measures in housekeeping applied correctly
Understanding of housekeeping operations demonstrated appropriately
Skills in interior and exterior decorations developed creatively

units.title: Form 1: Introduction to Laundry Processes
learningOutcomes: Practice laundry processes
suggestedActivities: Identifying fibers and fabric (natural fibers, synthetic fibers, blended fibers)
Discussing laundry equipment (washing, drying and finishing equipment)
Making use of laundry materials (detergents, fabric softeners, stain removers, bleach, whitening agents)
Demonstrating household laundry (care labels, laundry process, special care)
assessmentMethods: Laundry in the home practiced appropriately

units.title: Form 1: Cosmetology
learningOutcomes: Demonstrate understanding of the history of cosmetology
Demonstrate understanding of cosmetology practices
Make use of tools and equipment in cosmetology
suggestedActivities: Discussing the background of cosmetology (ancient civilizations, middle ages, renaissance, modern and contemporary eras)
Discussing components of cosmetology (nail technology, barbering, cosmetic chemistry and fitness)
Explaining terminologies used in cosmetology and the importance of cosmetology for personal development
Identifying hair, makeup and nail tools and equipment (cutting tools, colouring tools, styling brushes, sponges)
Sanitizing and sterilizing esthetic equipment and tools
assessmentMethods: Understanding of cosmetology demonstrated accordingly
Tools and equipment in cosmetology made use of correctly

units.title: Form 1: Entrepreneurship
learningOutcomes: Identify business opportunities
suggestedActivities: Discussing entrepreneurship
Describing qualities of an entrepreneur (risk taking, disciplined)
Exploring business opportunities (selling sweets, scones, pens, landscaping)
assessmentMethods: Business ideas explored extensively

units.title: Form 2: Introduction to Hospitality
topics: Economic Effect in the Hospitality Industry
learningOutcomes: Evaluate the effects of the hospitality industry on the economy
suggestedActivities: Discussing the effects of the hospitality industry on the economy (local economic development, small and medium enterprises, job creation, foreign exchange earnings, infrastructure development, tourism growth)
Undertaking an education tour to appreciate the benefit of the hospitality industry on the economy
assessmentMethods: Effects of the hospitality industry on the economy evaluated accordingly

units.title: Form 2: Front Office and Housekeeping Records
learningOutcomes: Apply skills to deliver exceptional front office services
Make use of records in the housekeeping department
suggestedActivities: Creating a mock scenario for front office desk customer service across the pre-arrival, arrival, occupancy and departure stages
Practicing social skills (posture, eye contact, poise and deportment, gestures)
Documenting housekeeping records (attendance book, handing over report, housekeeper's room report, lost and found book, room checklist)
Organizing records in the housekeeping department and conducting a mock audit of housekeeping records
assessmentMethods: Delivery of exceptional front office services applied skillfully
Records in the housekeeping department made use of correctly

units.title: Form 2: Housekeeping - Cleaning, Laundry and Maintenance
learningOutcomes: Apply correct cleaning processes
Perform laundry procedures in a hospitality facility
Conduct routine housekeeping maintenance
suggestedActivities: Identifying cleaning areas (guest rooms, corridors, public areas, conference rooms)
Using correct cleaning processes for different areas (sanitizing, sterilizing, disinfecting, polishing, mopping, vacuuming)
Demonstrating laundry procedures for guest linen (collecting and sorting, pretreatment, washing, drying, ironing, storage, distribution)
Conducting routine and preventive maintenance (clean and sanitize guest rooms, check and restock mini bar, test emergency lighting systems)
assessmentMethods: Cleaning processes applied skillfully
Laundry procedures performed accordingly
Routine housekeeping maintenance carried out successfully

units.title: Form 2: Food and Beverage Services
learningOutcomes: Create different menus
Cater for different functions
Carry out the basic sequence in food and beverage services
Show skillful serving of food and beverages
Cost food and beverage
Employ various food commodities in cookery
suggestedActivities: Discussing functions of the menu and identifying types of menus (table d'hote, a la carte, carte du jour, cyclical menu, static menu, banqueting menu)
Discussing menu planning considerations (seasonality, budget, dietary requirements, preparation time, variety)
Coordinating event catering logistics and preparing indigenous foods depicting a ceremony from a province in Zambia
Illustrating the basic sequence of service (pre-service, service, post-service)
Displaying table setting skills (formal and informal) and practicing serving of food using different styles of service (plated, assisted, self service)
Costing food and beverages (menu planning, recipe development, ingredient and dish costing)
Classifying food commodities (animal foods, plant foods, oils and fats, condiments, sugar and sweeteners, beverages, groceries)
assessmentMethods: Different menus created effectively
Catering for functions carried out effectively
Basic sequence of service carried out accordingly
Serving of food and beverages shown skillfully
Costing of food and beverages done correctly
Various food commodities employed in cookery correctly

units.title: Form 2: Cosmetology - Hair Care
learningOutcomes: Care of hair and scalp
Apply hair dye and colouring techniques
suggestedActivities: Explaining hair anatomy and discussing hair properties (texture, porosity, elasticity)
Identifying hair and scalp problems (dandruff, hair fall, dry and damaged hair, oily scalp)
Practicing hair care (shampooing, plaiting, barbing, locking)
Differentiating hair colour and dye products and applying hair colour
Streaking hair (horizontal, vertical, diagonal, balayage) and discussing advantages and disadvantages of streaking
assessmentMethods: Hair and scalp cared for skillfully
Hair dye and colouring techniques applied artfully

units.title: Form 2: Entrepreneurship
learningOutcomes: Set up a business venture
suggestedActivities: Exploring different types of businesses (partnership, sole trader, limited business)
Explaining the requirements for setting up a business (pre-operational requirements, business plan, registration and licensing, financial and location requirements)
Discussing regulatory compliance, health and safety requirements
assessmentMethods: Business ventures set up successfully

units.title: Form 3: Health and Safety
learningOutcomes: Apply safety protocols and procedures in the hospitality facility
Exhibit compliance with regulations and laws
suggestedActivities: Identifying causes of health hazards (poor lighting, inadequate ventilation, chemical exposure, poor ergonomics)
Applying first aid (save life, give assurance, transport)
Making use of safety equipment used in the hospitality industry (PPE, kitchen safety equipment, cleaning and disinfecting equipment)
Applying the occupational health and safety policy (purpose and scope, responsibilities, hazard identification and risk management)
Demonstrating a fire drill (pre-drill briefing, drill scenario, evacuation procedure)
assessmentMethods: Safety protocols and procedures applied correctly
Compliance with regulations and laws exhibited correctly

units.title: Form 3: Food Safety
learningOutcomes: Implement a food safety plan
Prevent food spoilage
suggestedActivities: Identifying types of food contamination (biological, chemical, physical, cross contamination)
Inspecting a kitchen to identify potential food safety hazards
Practicing proper hand washing techniques
Implementing a food safety plan that minimises risks
Explaining the causes and symptoms of food spoilage and researching strategies to prevent it (canning, freezing, vacuum sealing)
assessmentMethods: Food safety plan implemented successfully
Food spoilage prevented successfully

units.title: Form 3: Customer Care
topics: Competitive Edge in a Hospitality Facility
learningOutcomes: Deliver exceptional customer service
Apply customer care service
Maintain a competitive edge in a hospitality facility
Exhibit interpersonal skills in good customer care
suggestedActivities: Identifying types of customers and their specific needs
Assessing ways of dealing with different customers including those with special needs
Profiling customers (demographic, psychographic, behavioral personas)
Illustrating how unconscious bias can affect customer service
Performing tailored service and carrying out research to assess customer satisfaction
Identifying and engaging in interpersonal skills (listening skills, teamwork, conflict resolution) and practicing mediating skills
assessmentMethods: Exceptional service delivered successfully
Customer care service applied skillfully
Competitive edge in customer service maintained efficiently
Retention levels of interpersonal skills in customer care exhibited correctly

units.title: Form 3: Etiquette and Consumerism
learningOutcomes: Apply acceptable etiquettes in tourism and hospitality
Demonstrate understanding of consumerism
suggestedActivities: Discussing cultural differences and their impact on tourism experiences
Handling complaints (active listening, apologizing sincerely, offering solutions)
Applying table etiquette
Discussing consumerism (economic, social, environmental, cultural aspects)
Identifying consumer rights and responsibilities and describing organizations that protect consumers
assessmentMethods: Acceptable etiquette in tourism and hospitality applied correctly
Understanding of consumerism demonstrated correctly

units.title: Form 3: Cosmetology - Makeup and Facial Skin Care
learningOutcomes: Apply basic makeup
Map faces according to desired shape
Implement customized treatment for facial skin types
suggestedActivities: Identifying the problems associated with makeup and describing its importance
Applying makeup (priming, foundation, concealing, highlighting, contouring)
Identifying different shapes of faces (oval, round, square, heart-shaped, triangular, diamond)
Illustrating the steps of contouring and highlighting for different faces
Identifying skin types and preparing the skin (cleansing, exfoliating, toning, moisturizing)
Treating normal, dry, oily and combination skin types
assessmentMethods: Basic makeup applied correctly
Desired facial mapping worked out successfully
Customized treatment for facial skin types implemented correctly

units.title: Form 3: Professional Conduct
learningOutcomes: Practice professionalism in a hospitality facility
Maintain confidentiality in a hospitality facility
suggestedActivities: Discussing the importance of professional conduct in the hospitality industry (satisfaction, reputation, trust)
Role playing professional conduct (greeting a guest, taking a food order, handling a room service request)
Identifying confidential information (room numbers, credit card transactions)
Performing guest privacy drills and discussing consequences of breaching confidentiality
assessmentMethods: Professionalism in a hospitality facility practiced effectively
Confidentiality upheld successfully

units.title: Form 3: Communication
learningOutcomes: Apply different types of communication skills
Recognise barriers to communication in a hospitality facility
suggestedActivities: Practising written communication skills (notices, memorandum, letters)
Practising the use of technological communication (emails, social media, electronic business, online reservations)
Exploring common barriers to communication (language, cultural, noise and distractions, emotional)
Illustrating effective communication (cultural awareness, active listening, non-judgmental attitude, patience)
assessmentMethods: Communication skills applied effectively
Barriers to communication in a hospitality facility recognised efficiently

units.title: Form 3: Conflict Resolution and Security
learningOutcomes: Resolve conflicts in communication
Create a safe environment for guests and staff
suggestedActivities: Analyzing current conflicts and communication strategies
Creating cards with conflict scenarios and responding with effective communication strategies
Creating a security plan for a hypothetical establishment (assessment, security protocols, guest and staff safety, asset protection)
Participating in scenario-based training exercises to practice security response (active shooter, fire evacuation, communication protocols)
assessmentMethods: Conflicts in communication resolved amicably
Safe environment for guests and staff created suitably

units.title: Form 3: Entrepreneurship
topics: Security Risks in a Hospitality Facility
learningOutcomes: Mitigate security risks in a hospitality facility
Develop a business plan
suggestedActivities: Discussing potential security risks in a hospitality facility (theft, violence, cyber security, property damage)
Developing security mitigation strategies (risk assessment protocol, access control, emergency preparedness)
Developing a business plan for any business of choice from any component of hospitality management
Nurturing and growing an identified business venture
assessmentMethods: Security risks in a hospitality facility mitigated successfully
Business plan developed concisely

units.title: Form 4: Components of the Hospitality Industry
topics: Relationships Between Sub Divisions in a Hospitality Facility
learningOutcomes: Establish relationships between the subdivisions in hospitality
suggestedActivities: Explaining the relationship between the subdivisions in hospitality (front office and housekeeping, front office and food and beverage, housekeeping and maintenance, events and banqueting)
Designing a themed event and coordinating how each department contributes to the event's success
Creating service guidelines for interdepartmental relationships (protocols, request and task management, feedback and evaluation)
assessmentMethods: Relationships between the subdivisions of hospitality established accordingly

units.title: Form 4: Principles of Food Preparation
learningOutcomes: Display cooking methods and techniques
suggestedActivities: Preparing three course meals (mise en place, appetiser, entree, dessert)
Cooking using various techniques and methods (frying, roasting, steaming)
Plating the food considering size, occasion, visual appeal, balance and harmony (portion control)
Creating a mock buffet setup in the classroom
assessmentMethods: Cooking methods and techniques displayed correctly

units.title: Form 4: Gastronomy
learningOutcomes: Present culinary styles of cuisines
Apply the techniques of sous vide
Utilise cultural uniqueness and their cuisines
Reinvent indigenous dishes
Apply food preservation methods
suggestedActivities: Displaying culinary skills using nouvelle techniques to prepare different cuisines and traditional dishes
Preparing different styles of cuisines (Mediterranean, oriental, classical, fusion, haute, molecular)
Discussing sous vide cooking and demonstrating how to set up the sous vide machine, vacuum seal ingredients and cook at controlled temperature and time
Researching the relationship between food and Zambian culture and discussing culinary highlights of different regions
Preserving different foods using suitable methods (heat treatment, sterilization, pasteurization, sun drying, smoking, salting, bottling) and adding value to food
assessmentMethods: Culinary styles of cuisines presented skillfully
Techniques of sous vide applied correctly
Cultural uniqueness and their cuisines utilised accordingly
Indigenous dishes reinvented successfully
Food preservation methods applied skillfully

units.title: Form 4: Management
learningOutcomes: Demonstrate understanding of management
Manage an event
Organise banquets
suggestedActivities: Explaining the concept of management (planning, staffing, organizing) and its importance in hospitality operations
Discussing the benefits of good management (improved productivity, enhanced quality)
Discussing key elements in events (event budget, event timeline, venue selection) and exploring event planning stages (definition, preparation, implementation, evaluation)
Demonstrating events planning skills by planning school events
Discussing and planning banquets (logistics planning, menu and beverages)
Applying cost control (food cost control, sales control) and purchasing considerations (right source, right quality, right quantity, right time, right price)
assessmentMethods: Understanding of management demonstrated correctly
Events managed effectively
Banquets organized effectively

units.title: Form 4: Cosmetology - Nail Care, Barbering and Spa Services
learningOutcomes: Apply skills in nail care
Apply barbering skills
Apply spa services
suggestedActivities: Discussing nail anatomy and identifying nail disorders (trauma, ingrown toenails, fungal infections)
Performing basic manicure and pedicure and demonstrating proper techniques for shaping, cuticle care and polish application
Applying nail tips or overlays to enhance nail shape and appearance
Demonstrating proper sanitation and safety procedures and proficiency in cutting and shaving techniques (straight cut, diagonal cut, wet shaving, dry shaving)
Mastering fading and tapering techniques (gradual transitions, feathering, layering, blending)
Identifying the importance of spas and discussing spa services (massage therapy, body treatments)
assessmentMethods: Skills in nail care applied artfully
Barbering skills applied artfully
Spa services applied correctly

metadata.source: Hospitality Management Syllabus, Secondary Education Ordinary Level, Form 1-4
metadata.authority: Curriculum Development Centre (CDC), Ministry of Education, Republic of Zambia
metadata.extractionNote: Transcribed from the official CDC PDF. The PDF's embedded font ships no ToUnicode map and no space glyphs, so text was recovered by decoding the font's fixed +29 character offset and re-segmenting words from the syllabus tables (TOPIC / SUBTOPIC / SPECIFIC COMPETENCIES / LEARNING ACTIVITIES / EXPECTED STANDARDS). Content is transcribed from the source, not paraphrased or generated.
